title: Sudanese Political Factions Escalate Boycott of Third Berlin Conference on Sudan
detail: Multiple Sudanese political factions have escalated their boycott of the Third Berlin Conference on Sudan, scheduled for April 15, 2026 in Berlin — co-hosted by Germany and the African Union. The National Forces Alliance (NFA) and the National Forces Coordination both announced formal boycotts, citing unfair invitation distribution, the exclusion of the SAF-aligned Port Sudan government from a formal seat, and accusations that the conference framework had been 'hijacked' to serve interests opposed to Sudanese sovereignty. Preparatory talks are set for Addis Ababa on April 10–12. Analysts tracking the conference noted significant disinformation campaigns targeting the event. The conference was intended to produce a framework for ceasefire negotiations and civilian political transition, but growing boycotts risk undermining its legitimacy before it convenes. The SAF-aligned government has been ambivalent toward the conference, seeing it as a potential platform for civilian factions backed by Qatar and Western states to dilute SAF's political position.

title: 40,000+ Newly Displaced from El Fasher Area Amid Intensified RSF Attacks
detail: Over 40,000 people — approximately 8,100 households — have been forced to flee areas around El Fasher in North Darfur as RSF forces continued military operations in and around the city following the RSF's October 2025 capture. The displacement surge reflects ongoing RSF pressure on El Fasher's remaining civilian population, which has sought shelter in overcrowded displacement sites with severely limited humanitarian access. The UN Fact-Finding Mission, in a February 2026 report, documented that RSF forces killed 6,000+ civilians in the first three days of the El Fasher offensive, with over 4,400 killed within El Fasher itself — findings characterized as bearing the 'hallmarks of genocide' targeting non-Arab Zaghawa and Fur communities. Humanitarian agencies warn that access to El Fasher remains critically constrained, with aid supplies dwindling and medical facilities overwhelmed. The displacement adds to the already catastrophic humanitarian situation in North Darfur, where El Fasher was the last major city under non-RSF control before its October 2025 fall.
